text:
REGIME — Broad risk-on rally, tech and industrials led, energy crushed.

INDICES — VIX 24.5, falling. QQQ +1.24%, SPY +0.75%, IWM +0.63%, DIA +0.49%. Large caps outperformed small caps today. GLD surged +1.75% as the standout cross-asset move while TLT fell -0.50% and USO dropped -2.48%.

SECTORS — XLI +1.67%, XLK +1.51%, XLB +0.98%, XLV +0.76%, XLY +0.75%, XLU +0.48%, XLC +0.34%, XLRE +0.29%, XLF +0.14%, XLP -0.63%, XLE -3.74%. Top industries: GDX +4.62% and SILJ +3.67%; bottom: XOP -3.84% and FCG -3.34%.

THE 7s — Fab 7 +4.12%, Old Metals 7 +3.90%, Semi 7 +3.13%, Defense 7 +2.52%, Buildout 7 +2.27%, Industrial 7 +2.16%, New Metals 7 +1.77%, Bank 7 +0.94%, Health 7 +0.94%, Housing 7 +0.78%, Freight 7 +0.64%, Cloud 7 +0.59%, Power 7 +0.51%, New Staples 7 +0.36%, Medtech 7 +0.01%, Insurance 7 -0.26%, Software 7 -0.46%, Toll Road 7 -0.51%, Old Staples 7 -0.84%, Energy 7 -3.59%. Strongest ticker in Fab 7 not specified in data; weakest in Energy 7 not individually named.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed neutral at 50.1. Top movers: GDX +4.62% (Sectors/Industries), GREK +3.33% (vol spike, 3.1x), SILJ +3.67% (Industries). 20d momentum best sector: XLE +5.63%; worst: XLP -6.01%. Best basket: Semi 7 +4.26%; worst: Housing 7 -9.85%. Powell's comments downplaying Fed control over supply shocks eased tightening fears, directly lifting XLK +1.51% and dragging rate-sensitive XLE -3.74% on demand concerns.

WATCH — RSPU, +0.55%, 10.2x average volume with minimal price move signals potential positioning shift worth monitoring. DBC, -0.93%, 4.2x vol spike alongside commodity weakness raises commodity trend break risk. ARGT, -0.12%, 2.5x vol on flat price amid risk-on day is a notable divergence.
